briefGoals: G1 Thistlethwaite/Kociemba/Korf; G2 exact distance recognition; G3 heuristic for A*/IDA*
coverageAssessment: Coverage by the 16 reviewers is strong on the core math (cube model, coordinates, symmetry, PDBs, search, native solver, admissibility) and on artifact tracing, but six concrete holes remain that a strict supervisor would hit. (1) The end-to-end 3x3 subsystem is thesis-cited and completely unreviewed: src/rubik_optimal/solvers/end_to_end.py, scripts/run_3x3_end_to_end.py, results/processed/e2e_3x3_seed_2026_thesis.json and thesis/tables/e2e_3x3_status.tex feed an explicit section of chapter 05 (05_experiments.tex lines 41-45); the experiments and results-integrity reviewers both explicitly skipped it. (2) Eleven thesis files were read by nobody: 00_front_matter, 01_introduction (which has an uncommitted 5-line diff adding the own-code superflip-proof claim to the contributions list), 02_background, 03_cube_model, 04_literature_review, 05_system_design, 08_ai_disclosure, and appendices a_reproducibility, b_result_schema, c_submission_checklist, d_cli_reference - the reproducibility appendix matters most given the confirmed finding that scripts/thesis_audit.py currently fails its own acceptance gate, and nobody checked whether thesis/main.pdf was rebuilt from the current modified sources (thesis_audit's PDF check only compares root main.pdf vs thesis/main.pdf byte-identity, not freshness). (3) src/rubik_optimal/cli.py (1,832 lines) plus runtime.py and source_state.py were never read, despite a dedicated CLI-reference appendix the supervisor can execute; the provenance tags every artifact carries come from the unread source_state.py. (4) The certificate-cache closure scripts (run_certificate_cache_symmetry_closure.py, run_certificate_cache_inverse_closure.py, run_universal_symmetry_oracle.py) were explicitly skipped by the symmetry reviewer, and oracle.py lines ~3329-3500/3872-4496 by the distance-oracle reviewer - a wrong symmetry/inverse move-remapping there would inject wrong 'exact' certificates into the 7,562-entry store that other results consume. (5) The h48/oracle benchmark scripts behind the thesis headline speedups (11.56x, 189.894x, 38.198x, 40.16x) were never read by anyone; both tracing reviewers verified the numbers match the artifacts but nobody checked the measurement methodology (fair baselines, preload/warm-cache accounting, n=1 timings). (6) The pocket-cube code (pocket/cube.py, optimal.py, tables.py, solvers/pocket_cube.py, scripts/generate_pocket_cube.py) was reviewed only black-box via the distribution test, while the introduction leans on it as the 'complete exhaustive example' answering research question 4. Trivia not worth tasks: docs/supervisor_questions.md (+12) and solvers/__init__.py (+2, new exports) diffs unreviewed; tests/test_kociemba_phase2_native_probe.py unread but executed green by run-tests; I inspected scripts/apply_final_metadata.py (benign front-matter templating) and scripts/generate_tables.py (thin wrapper over the reviewed tables/generation.py); native/kociemba_phase2_probe's two-phase search internals (~lines 2150-2890) were skipped by all reviewers but only feed artifacts whose proof flags are false and whose solutions were replay-verified, so I do not propose a task for them. G1/G2/G3 each have end-to-end evidence reviewed; the G1 Thistlethwaite description/reproducibility problem is already a confirmed critical finding needing a fix, not more review.

areas.summary: The core cube model is correct. The cubie move tables in cube.py match Kociemba's published CubieCube tables exactly, and I verified them beyond that by hand-deriving an independent facelet-level simulator from physical first principles: all 6 generators agree with the model as exact 54-facelet permutations on scrambled states (where solved-state comparison would hide errors), and 50 random 40-move words agree end-to-end. Every quarter turn has order 4, every half turn order 2, move+inverse is identity on scrambled states, opposite faces commute, (R U) has order 105, (R U R' U')^6 is identity, and the standard distance-20 superflip generator produces exactly the all-edges-flipped/identity-permutation state. verify_physical correctly enforces twist sum mod 3, flip sum mod 2, parity equality, and piece-multiset checks, rejecting all corruption classes I threw at it; from_facelets round-trips on 500 random-walk states plus 500 uniformly sampled valid cubie states, and rejects sticker-valid-but-unsolvable states (single flip, single twist, lone 2-swap) as well as mirrored/malformed inputs (belt-and-braces: verify_physical plus a to_facelets round-trip guard). deterministic_scramble is reproducible across processes and PYTHONHASHSEED values, and the 44 tests in tests/test_moves_and_cube.py pass. The only issues found are minor: the scrambler is a random-move walk (not random-state) that permits reducible same-axis A-B-A patterns, and from_text has a theoretical 54-token parse ambiguity. Trivia folded here: half_turn_length (moves.py:57) is a plain token count (correct for HTM, slightly misleading name); `co[pos] = ori % 3` at cube.py:149 is a redundant mod; CubeState's constructor does not validate field lengths/types itself, but verify_physical catches malformed vectors.
areas.coverage: Reviewed in full: src/rubik_optimal/cube.py, src/rubik_optimal/moves.py, src/rubik_optimal/scramble.py, src/rubik_optimal/validity.py, tests/test_moves_and_cube.py (intent only), plus benchmark.py lines 30-70 and thesis/chapters/05_experiments.tex grep to assess how scrambles are labeled/claimed. Ran executable probes: move orders, inverse identities, opposite-face commutation, group-element orders (RU=105, sexy^6), superflip oracle, independent hand-derived facelet simulator compared as permutations per move and over 50x40-move words, facelet round-trips (500 random-walk + 500 uniformly sampled valid cubie states), rejection of 4 unsolvable-state classes and mirrored-corner stickers, scramble determinism under PYTHONHASHSEED=1 vs 999, A-B-A reducibility statistics over 300 scrambles, and pytest tests/test_moves_and_cube.py (44 passed). NOT reviewed (out of scope): coordinates/, symmetry.py, distance.py, oracle.py, solvers/, tables/, native C++ code, the rest of benchmark.py, experiment scripts, and exhaustive Greek-language thesis text auditing for randomness claims (only chapter 05 was grepped).

areas.summary: The coordinate layer is correct. I verified from first principles, by running probes: (1) encode/decode is an exact bijection over every documented domain — corner orientation 2187, edge orientation 2048, UD-slice 495, corner permutation 40320 (all 40320 decoded permutations distinct), phase-2 UD-edge permutation 40320, phase-2 slice permutation 24 exhaustively, edge permutation 12! by 5000-sample plus boundaries; (2) rank_permutation/unrank_permutation match the lexicographic index of itertools.permutations exhaustively for n=3..5 and sampled for n=8; (3) the cube model passes hard oracles (superflip generator yields eo=all-1 with identity permutations/twists; all move orders correct), and coord(apply(move,s)) == move_table[coord(s)][move] holds for 300 random valid scrambles x 18 moves x all phase-1 coordinates; (4) all six cached thesis_seed_2026 move tables in data/generated/ are byte-identical to full fresh rebuilds (including both 40320-row phase-2 tables compared in full, not sampled), and all cached pruning tables equal a fresh BFS with zero unreachable entries; the CO projection max of 6 and the corner-PDB max of 11 match published reference values; (5) the corner PDB binary indexed through corner_state_coord is consistent (solved=0, all 18 one-move states=1, |d(s)-d(s·m)|<=1 over 900 random neighbor pairs, superflip corners=0), so the Python and native index conventions agree; (6) solved-coordinate conventions (UD-slice solved=494, not 0) are consumed via SPEC.solved_coord everywhere including thistlethwaite_tables.py:392; (7) move-column ordering is consistent between build_move_table, the cache validation in solvers/kociemba.py (which rejects files whose 'moves' list differs), and every enumerate(ALL_MOVES/PHASE2_MOVES) consumer; (8) phase-1 heuristic pruning tables are built over all 18 moves, hence admissible for HTM, and the restricted phase-2 tables are never mixed into the full-metric heuristic; (9) _commuting_order_violation only prunes opposite-face same-axis pairs in non-canonical order, so the exact-depth G1 enumeration in kociemba_optimal.py is complete. The existing 9-test suite tests/test_coordinates_and_tables.py passes. Trivia folded here: decoded coordinate representatives carry default (solved) values for the unencoded cubie vectors, and search/heuristics.py:_projection_pruning_tables rebuilds tables in-memory instead of reusing the checksummed disk cache in solvers/kociemba.py (verified equal, so only a CPU-cost/code-duplication issue, not a correctness one).
areas.coverage: Reviewed and executed probes against all of src/rubik_optimal/coordinates/ (corner_orientation.py, edge_orientation.py, corner_permutation.py, edge_permutation.py, permutation.py, ud_slice.py, phase2.py, __init__.py), src/rubik_optimal/tables/move_tables.py, tables/pruning_tables.py, tables/generation.py, tables/corner_pdb.py (coordinate-index boundary only), and the coordinate-consuming paths in solvers/kociemba.py, solvers/kociemba_optimal.py, search/heuristics.py (lines 1-110), search/ida_star.py (_commuting_order_violation), plus the cube model (cube.py, moves.py) as the substrate. Ran: exhaustive bijection checks, Lehmer-rank-vs-lexicographic verification, move-table commutation on random valid states, full byte-equality of all six cached thesis_seed_2026 move/pruning tables vs fresh rebuilds, corner-PDB neighbor-consistency probe, phase-2 guard probes, and pytest tests/test_coordinates_and_tables.py (9 passed). NOT reviewed: the native C++ generators themselves (native/corner_pdb, native/edge_pdb, optimal_solver) beyond the Python-side index-convention probe; tables/edge_pdb.py subset-edge ranking; pocket/ (2x2) coordinates; symmetry.py coordinate symmetrization; thistlethwaite_tables.py beyond solved-coordinate convention spot checks; the 12!-domain edge permutation exhaustively (sampled only — full enumeration infeasible); and the optimality-proof logic of the solvers beyond their coordinate indexing.

areas.summary: The symmetry layer is mathematically sound and I could not break it despite adversarial probing. Verified from first principles: the 24/48 symmetry sets form groups (closure, inverses, matrix-vs-facelet action composition agree); the conjugation homomorphism S(s·m)=S(s)·S(m) holds for all 48 symmetries x 18 moves on random states (so move maps really implement S*m*S^-1, half-turns map to half-turns); g1_preserving_symmetries() returns exactly the 16 UD-axis-stabilizing symmetries (identity first) and all 16 map 300 random G1 states into G1 while each of the other 32 violates G1. The shipped FlipUDSlice reduction (data/generated/phase1_sym_tables.bin, sha256 matches its JSON) was re-verified against live conjugation of real reachable cubes: classidx/flipudslice_sym/classidx_to_rep/class_stab_mask/sym_twist all correct, class count 64,430 matches Kociemba's literature value, twist conjugation is independent of corner permutation (400 states x 16 syms), and a Python replica of the native sym_phase1_reduced_index (including the stabilizer-coset twist minimum) maps every member of each 16-symmetry orbit to one identical reduced index (300 orbits, 0 splits) — lookups correctly conjugate the raw coordinates before the table lookup. The BFS pruning cache's visited count (138,639,780) exactly equals the number of canonical reduced indices, so the 0xff/max+1 fallback is unreachable for real states (no hidden inadmissibility). Distances match a Python BFS of the raw phase-1 graph to depth 5 (95,039 coords, 0 mismatches); I re-ran the native verify-sym-phase1 gate and reproduced 959,761,462 compared entries vs the raw depth-9 BFS table with 0 mismatches; superflip phase-1 distance = 10 was pinned independently (raw entry >9, a neighbor at 9). The full-48 root-move quotient used by optimal-ida and the PDB IDA* is exact-safe including its interaction with canonical move ordering (U-face is first in opposite-face pairs in both native searches), and the narrower G1-preserving root quotient is correctly used for the fixed-axis two-phase driver. Python ALL_MOVES order matches native kMoveNames, and the three-axis conj-map direction is correct. Remaining findings are process/documentation-level, not correctness bugs.
areas.coverage: Reviewed in full: src/rubik_optimal/symmetry.py (incl. git diff HEAD — all additions), scripts/generate_phase1_sym_tables.py, data/generated/phase1_sym_tables.json/.bin (parsed byte-for-byte, sha verified), tests/test_symmetry.py (ran: 8 passed) and tests/test_kociemba_phase1_symmetry.py (ran 3 of 14 native tests incl. the optimal-ida admissibility gate: passed); the sym-table loading/lookup/BFS/verify/optimal-ida/three-axis sections of native/kociemba_phase2_probe/kociemba_phase2_probe.cpp (lines ~1555-1835, 2040-2150, 2850-3060, 3790-3870); root-mask and move-ordering code in native/optimal_solver/optimal_solver.cpp; usages in solvers/optimal_native.py and the probe scripts; symmetry-related thesis claims in 04_implementation/05_experiments/06_validation and docs/limitations.md. Ran heavy probes: group/homomorphism checks (48x18x5 states), G1-preservation (300 states x 16 syms + 32 negatives), table-vs-live-state conjugation (400 random reachable states), orbit invariance of the reduced index (300x16), canonical-index census vs BFS visited count, depth-5 BFS distance cross-check, and the full native verify-sym-phase1 raw gate (959,761,462 entries, 18 s). NOT reviewed: the two-phase driver internals (phase-2 handoff/dedup/threading — another reviewer's area), the raw phase-1 full BFS builder internals (trusted via the cross-checks above), the remaining 11 native tests in test_kociemba_phase1_symmetry.py, nissy/h48 backends, edge_projection fullsym probes, and the run_certificate_cache_symmetry_closure / universal_symmetry_oracle scripts.

areas.summary: The committed Thistlethwaite implementation (table-guided greedy descent over four exact BFS coset tables) is mathematically correct. Verified from first principles and by experiment: the EO convention (F/B quarter turns flip eo) is consistent with G1=<U,D,L,R,F2,B2>; G2 entry = CO=0 + the four E-slice edges in slice (EO preserved by the G1 move set); G3 membership uses exact square-group corner/edge projections (96 x 6912 = 663,552, and the g3 BFS reaches all 663,552, proving the direct-product factorization the phase-3 coset quotient relies on). Each stage uses the correct move set (18/14/10/6); table max depths are 7/10/13/15 matching the published phase bounds; g2 has 29,400 reachable cosets (the parity-consistent half of 420x140). All four cached binaries are byte-identical to in-process regeneration, the coset signature matches the manifest, independently re-derived BFS distances match table values, left-coset invariance of g2_index holds on 50 random (h,x) pairs, and the classic trap (corner 3-cycle within a tetrad: orbits and parity OK but outside the square group) is correctly rejected. 20 end-to-end solves (incl. superflip) all verify, with stage boundaries landing in the right subgroups and stage lengths within 7/10/13/15 (totals 26-35). However, the thesis chapters and the saved benchmark artifacts describe and were produced by a DIFFERENT, older bounded-IDA* implementation: the thesis's implementation description is contradicted by the repository code, and re-running the benchmark with current code yields different solution lengths (one case 32 > the published max 27), runtimes, and node counts.
areas.coverage: Reviewed: src/rubik_optimal/solvers/thistlethwaite.py, src/rubik_optimal/tables/thistlethwaite_tables.py, scripts/generate_thistlethwaite_tables.py, supporting modules (cube.py move definitions, coordinates/edge_orientation.py, corner_orientation.py, ud_slice.py, verify.py), the cached tables + manifest in data/generated/thistlethwaite/, tests/test_search_and_solvers.py Thistlethwaite tests (ran: 19 passed), Thistlethwaite-related passages in thesis/chapters/03,04,05,06,07 and thesis/tables/*, and saved artifacts results/processed/benchmarks_seed_2026_thesis.csv and summary_seed_2026_thesis.json. Ran: full regeneration of all four tables with SHA-256 comparison against the cached manifest (all byte-identical); coset-signature recomputation vs manifest (match); 20 end-to-end random solves + superflip with independent move replay and per-stage subgroup-membership checks; independent re-derived BFS cross-checks of g0/g1 table exactness; 50-sample left-coset invariance check of g2_index; G3 trap-state probes; a tampered-manifest experiment in /tmp; re-ran three saved CSV scrambles through the current solver. NOT reviewed: benchmark.py/CLI harness internals, the Kociemba/Korf/H48 tracks, the Greek thesis text beyond Thistlethwaite passages, and I did not exhaustively validate every g2/g3 table entry (relied on byte-identical regeneration plus spot BFS cross-checks) nor regenerate the full benchmark CSV.

areas.summary: The Kociemba stack is in unusually good shape; I could not break any correctness or optimality claim despite adversarial probing. Verified by running code: (1) solve_kociemba_native_scoped on 12 random scrambles — every returned solution replays to solved, the phase-1 prefix lands exactly in G1 (CO/EO/slice solved), the phase-2 suffix uses only {U,U',U2,D,D',D2,R2,L2,F2,B2}, and no reducible same-face seam appeared (also none in the 26 saved thesis benchmark solutions, all 13+13 of which replay and are honestly labeled non_exact). (2) solve_kociemba_two_phase_optimal's "exact" status matched an independent meet-in-the-middle exact-distance oracle on 11 cases including pure-phase-2 (G1-input) scrambles and the F R U R' U' = 5 trap; its subgroup-decomposition optimality argument and its lower_bound-vs-timeout discipline are sound on code review (timeouts/node-limits always poison the proof). (3) All six cached coordinate move tables validate entry-by-entry against the cube model on random samples; pruning tables are exact BFS projections (solved=0, no -1 sentinels, 1-Lipschitz over move edges, admissible vs scramble length; the 24-entry slice table verified exhaustively; maxima 6/7/5 and 13/8/4 are plausible). (4) The native superflip evidence chain checks out: the saved 20-move two-phase solution replays to solved in the Python model; the bound-19 Reid optimal-ida artifact ran on the true superflip with root mask {U,U2}, no timeout/node-limit, proves_no_solution<=19, 5200.77 s (the thesis's "~87 minutes"); I independently verified the 48-element stabilizer, the conjugation identity T(c·m)=T(c)·T(m) for all 48 symmetries and for the RL/FB three-axis inputs, and orbit coverage of the {U,U2} mask; I re-ran the sym-table verify mode and reproduced 0 mismatches over 959,761,462 raw entries. Remaining findings are evidence-archival and robustness gaps, not wrong results.
areas.coverage: Reviewed and ran: src/rubik_optimal/solvers/kociemba.py, solvers/kociemba_optimal.py, coordinates/phase2.py, tables/pruning_tables.py, tables/move_tables.py, moves.py, verify.py, the relevant parts of search/ida_star.py (commuting-order pruning), search/heuristics.py (combined_table_lower_bound), and symmetry.py (root masks, three-axis inputs — verified empirically). Validated all kociemba_* cached JSON tables in data/generated against the cube model; re-ran the prebuilt native kociemba_phase2_probe in verify-sym-phase1 mode (read-only). Reviewed native/kociemba_phase2_probe/kociemba_phase2_probe.cpp for the optimal-ida mode, the two-phase proof flags, the sym-table build/lookup and the phase-2 move set; reviewed scripts/probe_native_kociemba_two_phase_superflip.py and the reid/two-phase/benchmark artifacts in results/. Ran 4 behavioral probes (solver replay/G1/move-set, MITM exactness oracle, table validation, symmetry identities). NOT reviewed: the native probe's two-phase search internals line-by-line (cp-target/cp-slice/ud-edge bounded target tables, threading, ~lines 2150-2890 — these only feed artifacts whose proof flags are false), scripts/generate_phase1_sym_tables.py internals, the external `kociemba` pip adapter beyond code reading, and the repo's own pytest suites (superseded by independent probes). No tables regenerated, nothing in the repo modified.

areas.summary: The Korf PDB stack is mathematically sound and the binary artifacts are correct. I verified from first principles that the native generators' move tables are identical to the Python cube model (all six faces' cp/co/ep/eo arrays match line by line), that the Lehmer permutation ranking and MSB-first base-3 orientation encoding agree between Python and C++, and that the 6/7-edge coordinate (combination-rank * N! + perm-rank) * 2^N + orientation uses the same scan-order convention on both sides. Empirically: the corner PDB histogram matches Korf's published distribution exactly (max 11, mean 8.764134, counts 1/18/243/2874/28000/.../64736); every PDB file (corner, eight 6-edge, two 7-edge, two cost-partitioned) is complete with 0 unvisited entries, which proves each index function is a bijection onto [0,N); PDB[solved]=0 for all 13 tables; a full-cube BFS to depth 4 showed every projected coordinate's .bin entry equals its true projected distance with per-depth counts matching the histograms (zero mismatches across corner, two 6-edge, and one 7-edge table); 2,300 admissibility probes (2,000 with exact known distance, 300 deep scrambles) found zero violations including superflip; the cost-partitioned PDBs' zero-cost layers exactly equal independently computed free-move orbits (4,032 and 48,384); and the native optimal solver returned exact, Python-verified optimal lengths on 11 probe states. The findings are about evidence-freshness and documentation, not correctness: the working tree now silently includes the new 7-edge PDBs in both the Python and native default heuristics, so re-running the frozen thesis evidence scripts would produce different node counts than the saved artifacts and contradict the thesis sentence that the native solver uses "the eight edge PDBs"; the scoped Korf solver also underreports table bytes by the 1.02 GB of 7-edge tables its heuristic actually consults; and stale "~13 max distance" comments contradict the measured value of 11.
areas.coverage: Reviewed: src/rubik_optimal/solvers/korf.py, search/ida_star.py (the search korf.py relies on, including the commuting-move prune and status semantics), search/heuristics.py, tables/corner_pdb.py, tables/edge_pdb.py (incl. git diff HEAD), native/corner_pdb/corner_pdb.cpp, native/edge_pdb/edge_pdb.cpp (incl. diff), scripts/generate_corner_pdb.py, scripts/generate_edge_pdb.py, the PDB-loading/heuristic-combination sections of native/optimal_solver/optimal_solver.cpp and solvers/optimal_native.py, all 13 PDB .bin files in data/generated/, the 7-edge metadata JSONs (sha256 checksums verified against the bins), the frozen evidence files optimal_3x3_seed_2026_thesis.json and korf_native_evidence_seed_2026_thesis_native_only.json, docs/limitations.md, and the PDB-related thesis claims in chapters 04/05. Ran: corner-PDB histogram vs Korf's published stats; completeness/bijection counts for all PDBs; depth-4 full-cube BFS equivalence check of Python encoders vs native-generated bins; 2,300-state admissibility probes incl. superflip; cpdb zero-layer orbit cross-check; 11 native-solver exactness probes verified independently in Python. NOT done: did not run the repo pytest suites for PDBs (they may recompile binaries into native/build, which the review rules forbid); did not regenerate any table; did not validate cost-partitioned BFS layers >= 1 beyond admissibility sampling; did not review the optimal solver's search internals (threads, transposition tables, symmetry pruning, nissy/h48 paths - other reviewers' scope); did not cross-check the 7-edge histogram against published 7-edge PDB statistics (only max/mean plausibility and the depth<=4 exact equivalence).

areas.summary: The search layer is sound. IDA* is textbook-correct: the first threshold is h(root) (ida_star.py:102,165), the next threshold is the minimum f that exceeded the current bound (lines 127-129, 158-159, 186), the goal test sits correctly after the f-bound check and before the depth/budget cutoffs, and timeout/node-limit paths never claim "exact". Empirically, on 21 random states of scramble length 1-7, IDA* with combined_table_lower_bound matched the native optimal solver's length exactly, every returned path replayed to solved, and results were identical with prune_commuting_moves on/off and with heuristic child ordering; 8 pathological same-axis states (U D, D U, U2 D2, R L U D, ...) matched a BFS oracle. The commuting prune removes exactly the 27 canonical ordered pairs, all genuinely commuting, never both orders of a pair. All heuristics combine via max() (combined == max(components) asserted per state; the native engine likewise takes a MAX, folds the cost-partitioned additive sum in admissibly via a correct 0-1 BFS generator, and rejects incomplete edge PDBs at load, optimal_solver.cpp:1329). Admissibility — the load-bearing property — held with zero violations on 8 native-oracle states (d=6..14) plus the superflip (d=20) for every full-distance heuristic exposed in heuristics.py; the PDB artifacts match published ground truth (corner PDB max 11, mean 8.7641 vs Korf's 8.764; 6-edge max 10; both 7-edge PDBs complete, max 11, zero unvisited entries). The symmetry layer backing conjugated lookups verified clean: 48-element group, conjugation homomorphism 0/3456 mismatches, three-axis conjugates are valid states preserving optimal distance, superflip stabilizer is 48 with first-move representatives (U, U2), and root-representative pruning preserved BFS-exact distances on symmetric states. The seven-edge gate tests (10/10 incl. native layers) pass and the saved artifact results/processed/seven_edge_strength_seed_2026.json matches the design doc's claims (superflip gain +0, mean random gain +0.50). I found no critical or major defects; the findings below are consistency/coverage issues a supervisor could trip on.
areas.coverage: Reviewed and ran: src/rubik_optimal/search/ida_star.py, bfs.py, heuristics.py (incl. git diff HEAD), src/rubik_optimal/tables/edge_pdb.py (coordinate encoder, loader, header validation), src/rubik_optimal/moves.py, src/rubik_optimal/distance.py and solvers/korf.py call sites, the native heuristic combination + PDB load guards + 0-1 BFS cost handling in native/optimal_solver/optimal_solver.cpp and native/edge_pdb/edge_pdb.cpp (read only the heuristic/load/generation sections), symmetry.py conjugation helpers (CUBE_SYMMETRIES, three_axis_phase1_inputs, root_symmetry_representative_moves), tests/test_search_and_solvers.py and tests/test_seven_edge_heuristic.py (executed: 70 passed / 14 skipped, all skips honest BFS-oracle-cap skips), scripts/measure_seven_edge_strength.py plus its saved JSON, docs/WORSTCASE_HEURISTIC_DESIGN.md, and binary PDB artifacts (full numpy histograms). Empirical probes (in /tmp): admissibility of all exposed heuristics vs native-oracle distances 6-14 + superflip; IDA* optimality/replay/prune-equivalence on 21 random + 8 symmetric states; commuting-prune table enumeration; symmetry homomorphism and distance preservation; BFS boundary behavior. NOT reviewed: the native IDA* engine's search loop/transposition tables/threading internals, the Reid 3-axis proof driver in native/kociemba_phase2_probe and the FlipUDSlice sym-table generation (only its Python conjugation inputs were probed), solvers/kociemba.py, kociemba_optimal.py, thistlethwaite.py, oracle.py, thesis LaTeX chapters (only grepped for 7-edge claims, none found), and no tables were regenerated.

areas.summary: The native Korf IDA* solver is sound and its optimality claims are well supported. I verified from first principles and empirically: (1) move encoding and state serialization agree exactly between Python and C++ — kBaseMoves in optimal_solver.cpp:118-125 is byte-identical to BASE_MOVES in src/rubik_optimal/cube.py, move-name order matches ALL_MOVES, and build_moves slots X/X'/X2 correctly; (2) the heuristic is an admissible MAX of corner PDB, 6-edge and new 7-edge PDBs (the bounded early-exit variant only short-circuits when a single admissible component already proves the prune, so IDA* threshold bookkeeping stays safe); (3) the search is plain iterative deepening with next-bound = min frontier f, same-face and opposite-face-order prunes, and a depth-keyed transposition table whose prune rule (stored depth <= g) is the standard sound one; (4) the new symmetry-transposition mode correctly disables the order-dependent opposite-face prune (should_skip_face_after_last, line 1432) so the remaining tree is invariant under face renaming, maps last_face through the transform, and the compact packed key (12!-rank | eo-bits | last_face, corner_coord) is injective on valid states; (5) the upper-bound proof path now distinguishes proof_active from proof_exhaustive so "exhaustive" is only reported after a completed search — an improvement over the previous code. Empirically: solved state returns 0; all 18 single-move states return length-1 solutions that replay to solved; 10 BFS-oracle scrambles match in three configurations (plain, 7-edge+symTT+compact, threads=4+full-sym, 30 runs); 10 random 8-10-move scrambles match the fully independent nissy 2.0.8 optimal solver (plus 4 full-symmetry compact-TT spot checks); upper-bound proof correctly finds the true optimum 5 given a suboptimal length-7 upper solution, certifies an optimal upper solution, and rejects a bogus one; root-symmetry masks preserve optimal lengths on 6 highly symmetric states; pytest test_optimal_native.py (17) and test_seven_edge_heuristic.py (10, incl. 25-state native-vs-Python coordinate cross-check) pass; both prebuilt binaries are newer than the source and the nissy-bridge binary solves correctly with the nissy heuristic enabled. The one sharp edge is that --root-move-mask lets the binary label provably non-optimal lengths as status "exact" (demonstrated), with soundness delegated entirely to the caller; current saved superflip artifacts honestly report timeout, so no existing claim is wrong.
areas.coverage: Reviewed in depth: native/optimal_solver/optimal_solver.cpp (full file plus the ~940-line uncommitted diff: 7-edge PDB support, emit-edge-coords hook, root move mask, 24/48-symmetry transposition canonicalization, compact packed TT, proof-active/exhaustive split, hardened edge-PDB header validation), src/rubik_optimal/solvers/optimal_native.py, tests/test_optimal_native.py, nissy_bridge.h/.c (incl. admissibility of the corner + three-axis DRUD bound with the all-equal +1 trick), the root-mask helper root_symmetry_representative_moves in symmetry.py (soundness argument only), and native/edge_projection_probe/edge_projection_probe.cpp (move tables, projection ranking, bidirectional BFS termination — exact; canonicalization used only for ball counting, not distances). Ran: solved/single-move cases, 30 BFS-oracle comparison runs, 10 independent nissy-optimal comparisons (+4 full-sym TT), upper-bound-proof probes, root-mask equivalence probes, both binaries, and the relevant pytest files. NOT reviewed: native/kociemba_phase2_probe/kociemba_phase2_probe.cpp beyond a skim of its optimal-ida driver and three_axis_solved_bound (its phase-1/phase-2 coordinate encodings, conj_rl/conj_fb axis maps, and sym-table reduction are unverified — belongs with the Kociemba-area review; statuses it emits are honestly labeled solution_found/lower_bound/timeout, never "exact"); the new solve_kociemba_two_phase_optimal solver; correctness internals of symmetry.py stabilizer computation (black-box validated only); superflip-class runs (too slow); the nissy-bridge corner-orientation conversion tables (k_corl_from_coud) beyond black-box solving; edge PDB generation code.

areas.summary: The distance-recognition stack is, with one significant exception, soundly engineered: every code path that emits status="exact" either (a) replays the returned move sequence on the repo's own cube model from the exact input state before setting is_verified (FastOptimalOracle, race/resident-race/portfolio/universal oracles, h48/nissy/nissy-core-direct/RubikOptimal backends, all rotated-variant paths map solutions back and re-verify on the original cube), or (b) is a genuine upper==lower certificate / completed bounded-search proof. Timeouts are consistently downgraded ("timeout"/"failed"/"lower_bound", never exact): subprocess.TimeoutExpired, resident select() timeouts (which also kill the session, preventing request/response misalignment), and the native h48_backend's completed_negative_search flag all behave conservatively. Live probes confirmed: recognize_distance returns exact 0/1/2/3 for known shallow states; the h48 native solver matches exhaustive BFS at depths 4-6; h48 single and 23-rotation lower bounds are admissible; the portfolio upper-lower certificate path certified a depth-6 state correctly; all 24 rotations preserve distance on a probe state; and a full replay audit of every saved exact row in results/processed (2,521 rows) found zero verification failures and zero expected-distance mismatches. The exception: optimality (as opposed to validity) of the imported nissy-core "known-distance benchmark" certificates is inherited purely from a third-party file label with no local proof, and these dominate the certificate cache (79%) and back 126 saved "exact" oracle rows — this is the one channel where the repo's own rule "never present unproven output as exact" is at risk. Remaining trust assumptions (vendored nissy-core's iterative-deepening invariant, external nissy 2.x "-o", RubikOptimal "(Nf*)") are disclosed in notes but never independently proven; replay only certifies validity, not minimality.
areas.coverage: Reviewed in full: src/rubik_optimal/distance.py, verify.py, exact_certificates.py; all status="exact" emission sites in oracle.py (enumerated by grep, each inspected: lines 375, 697, 888, 1001, 1125, 2503, 2650, 2862, 2943, 3756, 4569, 4821, 5182); solvers/h48_native.py (conversion, session protocol, batch timeout recovery, lower-bound single/rotational/multi-cube batch); solvers/nissy_external.py (nissy 2.x `solve optimal -o`, nissy-core direct `-O 0`, parsers, timeout paths); solvers/rubikoptimal_external.py (resident session, "(Nf*)" parsing, _result_from_row); native/h48_backend/h48_backend.c (solve/batch/lower-bound status logic, proved_lower_bound, deadline poll); native/optimal_solver/optimal_solver.cpp upper-bound-proof flow (single-bound search continues exhaustively after a found goal, so it is sound); search/ida_star.py threshold discipline (timeout/node-budget checked before reusing a truncated iteration's bound — sound). Scripts: import_nissy_benchmark_certificates.py, run_distance_recognition_corpus.py, run_universal_oracle_cli.py (status passthrough, no relabeling), run_known_distance20_trimmed_prepass_sweep.py (confirms --no-certificate-cache for thesis distance-20 rows), run_h48_resident_certification.py, run_learned_certificate_cache.py. Live probes run: recognize_distance on known distances 0-3; h48 native vs exhaustive BFS at depths 4/5/6 (all match); 24-rotation distance invariance; h48 lower-bound admissibility (4 states); portfolio upper-lower certificate end-to-end (exact 6, correct); nissy-core direct (exact 5, correct); certificate store load (7,562 certs, superflip found at 20, replay OK); replay audit of all 2,521 saved exact rows in results/processed (0 failures); pytest test_exact_certificates.py + test_h48_native.py (49 passed). NOT reviewed: oracle.py solve_many orchestration internals (lines 3329-3500, 3872-4496) and rubikoptimal rotational-race internals beyond pattern checks; NissyCoreDirectPythonSession internals (nissy_external.py 599-1060) beyond status-grep; kociemba adapter beyond its upper-bound verification gate; optimal_solver.cpp full search/PDB correctness and symmetry.py implementation (other reviewers' areas — I validated their distance-invariance empirically only); h48 table generation; superflip was NOT re-solved live (saved artifact row + local replay only).

areas.summary: The experimental pipeline is in very good shape and survived adversarial recomputation. Verified positives: (1) seed-2026 RNG plumbing is deterministic end to end - I regenerated every scramble and facelet state in results/raw/benchmarks_seed_2026_thesis.jsonl from deterministic_scramble(seed=2026) and they match exactly (random.Random(str) uses version-2 seeding, platform-stable); (2) all 11 benchmark tables, 4 figure-data files, the processed CSV and the summary JSON regenerate byte-identically from the saved raw rows via generate_benchmark_artifacts_from_saved_results, so the thesis tables provably come from the cited raw file; (3) per-solver medians, status counts, and verified counts in summary_seed_2026_thesis.json recompute correctly, and there is no survivorship bias - lower_bound/timeout rows are retained in the raw rows, in all tables, and in the runtime medians; (4) roughly 27 numeric claims in thesis/chapters/05_experiments.tex (batch overhead 69.74s/6.03s/11.56x, trusted speedup 36.64s/0.193s/189.894x, superflip certification 51.81s/158.44s, own-code superflip bound-19 proof 5200.8s with 11,203,278,121 expanded nodes, nissy-optimal 23.73s, portfolio 10.10s/135.68s/0.013s, etc.) all trace to saved result files; (5) cross-backend consistency holds (depth-15 case = 14 moves and depth-20 case = 17 moves from independent backends; scoped Korf 11/13 exact matches the text; shallow_6 distance 4 independently re-verified by BFS); (6) heuristic_comparison and edge-PDB-coverage means/flags recompute exactly (80 cases, 0 weaker, 14 improved); (7) pocket-cube (3,674,160 states, max 11) and 7-edge PDB metadata (510,935,040 entries each, max 11) match ground truth; (8) scripts/verify_results.py passes with zero errors. The thesis text honestly documents random-move-sequence (not random-state) sampling, nominal-depth-vs-distance caveats, and the small corpus size. The main real problem is that the repository's own audit gate (scripts/thesis_audit.py), which the thesis appendix tells the supervisor to run, currently fails because its required-source-path list points at six scripts that were moved to scripts/experimental/; the committed thesis_audit.json records acceptance_implementation_passed=false. There are also small command-vs-artifact provenance mismatches and a stdout-only diagnostic script with no saved artifact.
areas.coverage: Reviewed: scripts/run_benchmarks.py, scripts/run_3x3_optimal.py, scripts/measure_heuristic_strength.py, scripts/compare_heuristics.py, scripts/generate_figures.py, scripts/generate_superflip_proof_table.py (partially), scripts/thesis_audit.py (required-paths section), scripts/verify_results.py (ran it), src/rubik_optimal/benchmark.py, src/rubik_optimal/results.py, src/rubik_optimal/scramble.py; result files: benchmarks raw JSONL + CSV + summary, optimal_3x3 thesis/stress-h48h0/stress-h48h7-oracle/nissy-optimal, heuristic_comparison, edge_pdb_coverage (both), edge_pdb7 + per-subset 7-edge metadata, edge_projection probes (2 sampled), pocket cube distribution/summary, thesis_audit.json, h48 batch/trusted/resident/certification/CLI/portfolio files (numeric tracing); thesis/chapters/05_experiments.tex claims cross-checked against artifacts; regenerated all benchmark-derived tables in /tmp and diffed byte-for-byte; recomputed medians, means, admissibility flags, distribution sums; re-verified one solver solution and one BFS distance independently. NOT reviewed: solver/search implementation correctness (korf/kociemba/thistlethwaite/native C++, recognize_distance internals, superflip proof code soundness - only artifact tracing), the ~40 h48/nissy probe files not cited by the thesis, cloud_hardtail/aws dryrun artifact families, e2e/distance-recognition-corpus scripts, table generation scripts (generate_corner_pdb/edge_pdb generation logic), chapters other than 05 except by grep; did not rerun any benchmark or regenerate any table.

areas.summary: I cross-checked the technical claims in chapters 00/03/04/05/06/07 against the generated table fragments (thesis/tables/*.tex) and the saved artifacts in results/processed/. The large majority of quantitative claims trace cleanly and match: all H48 certification/batch/trusted/resident/CLI/API timings quoted in prose (51.813928, 44.591136, 158.440286, 69.740540/6.033133/11.56x, 36.643667/0.192970/189.894x, 4.015766/0.105130/38.198x, 5.917467/5.853855, 2.132448, 4.638556/0.115502/40.16x, 72.071228/77.822389/154.755697, 2.664585, 91.629342, 10.097333/135.679618/0.012885, 23.734412) were found verbatim in the corresponding saved JSON/tex artifacts; PDB sizes match the combinatorics (8!*3^7=88,179,840 corner with max 11; eight 6-edge PDBs of 42,577,920 = 340,623,360 total, max 10; CPDB 85,155,840, max dist 3; coverage 80 cases/14 improved; heuristic comparison 7 shallow + 6 deep with admissibility flags true); the Pocket Cube distribution sums to exactly 3,674,160 with the canonical HTM distribution (max 11, 2,644 antipodes); coordinate-space sizes and the Thistlethwaite/Kociemba/Korf descriptions are consistent with the literature facts. For the central own-code superflip=20 claim I independently re-verified the length-20 two-phase solution against the repo cube model and the standard superflip generator, confirmed the bound-19/bound-18 proof artifacts (proves_no_solution_at_or_below_target=true, timed_out=false, 5200.77s ≈ the quoted 87 minutes, 11,203,278,121 expanded nodes), confirmed the FlipUDSlice metadata (64,430 classes, 2,033 nontrivial stabilizers, 16 symmetries), and reproduced the byte-for-byte sym-vs-raw validation myself (959,761,462 compared, 0 mismatches, 31s). Remaining issues are mostly traceability/wording: a false uniqueness claim in the validation chapter, two prose benchmark numbers with no saved artifact, a missing saved artifact for the heavily-quoted sym-table validation, and timeout/lower_bound terminology contradictions. Trivia folded here: 04_implementation:55 still says the repo has no committed state while the checkout now has 3 commits (artifacts still record "no_commit+dirty"); the working tree adds an optional 7-edge PDB path (heuristics.py/optimal_native.py, edge_pdb7_metadata.tex) not described in the frozen thesis text — fine if labeled post-thesis follow-up, otherwise the implementation chapter is slightly out of date.
areas.coverage: Reviewed: thesis/chapters/00_abstracts.tex, 03_algorithms.tex, 04_implementation.tex, 05_experiments.tex, 06_validation.tex, 06_discussion.tex, 07_conclusions.tex (current working-tree state); all table fragments cited by those chapters under thesis/tables/ and thesis/figures/ that carry numbers quoted in prose; results/processed/edge_pdb7_metadata.tex; ~25 saved JSON artifacts (superflip Reid target-18/19 proofs, phase1_sym_tables.json, depth-9 raw-table build, H48 certification/batch/trusted/resident/CLI/API/portfolio, nissy-optimal stress, heuristic comparison, edge/corner/h48 metadata); native verify-sym-phase1 source and the phase1 symmetry pytest file; Korf solver status logic. Ran two probes: (1) native verify-sym-phase1 byte-for-byte cross-check (reproduced 959,761,462 compared / 0 mismatches in 31s), (2) independent Python re-verification of the own-code length-20 superflip solution and of the superflip state encoding against the standard generator. NOT reviewed: chapters 01/02/03_cube_model/04_literature_review/05_system_design and appendices; raw JSONL benchmark rows row-by-row; soundness of the native C++ search code itself (other reviewers' scope); H48/nissy vendored backend internals; the ~200 uncited cloud/hardtail/universal-oracle fragments; no long runs re-executed (target-19 proof, H48 hard rows); thread-invariance of the proof node count not re-measured.

areas.summary: The suite (700 collected tests; 28 root files + 7 infrastructure files) is substantially better than typical thesis test suites: no assert-True / try-except-pass vacuities were found; the core optimality tests use an exhaustive BFS oracle as the sole ground truth (test_optimal_native.py, test_kociemba_optimal.py, test_search_and_solvers.py, test_kociemba_phase1_symmetry.py); negative-direction proofs exist (IDA* at bound-1 must prove no solution, test_kociemba_phase1_symmetry.py:283-306); the 2x2x2 path is anchored by a full 3,674,160-state enumeration matching the published distribution (test_pocket_cube.py:67-77); and mocked wrapper tests are honestly annotated as not proving optimality (test_optimal_native.py:17-22). The specific suspicion in the assignment is resolved in the author's favor: the "fix native-optimal test constant" replaced a hand-guessed expectation of 4 with a BFS oracle, and I independently confirmed F R U R' U' is exactly distance 5 (meet-in-the-middle probe), so the test was fixed, not rigged. On this machine all skip gates are open (compiler, native binaries, 6/7-edge PDBs, h48 tables, phase-1 sym tables), so green is real, not skip-driven; only ~15/30 randomized optimality cases skip by an explicit BFS-depth-4 cap. The real weaknesses are: (1) the giant "oracle contract" tests assert hundreds of substring-greps over source files plus self-reported "all_exact" flags from saved JSON artifacts without re-verifying anything; (2) live optimality/admissibility testing tops out at distance ~8, so all deep exactness (incl. superflip=20) rests on saved artifact flags; (3) the shipped full corner/edge PDB binaries that back every Korf optimality claim have no integrity or distribution test; and (4) the registered "infrastructure" marker is applied to zero tests, so the documented -m filtering is a no-op for the 301 mock-heavy plumbing tests (43% of the suite).
areas.coverage: Reviewed: tests/conftest.py and pyproject.toml pytest config; read in full test_optimal_native.py, test_search_and_solvers.py, test_kociemba_optimal.py, test_seven_edge_heuristic.py, test_kociemba_phase1_symmetry.py, test_edge_projection_probe.py, test_native_corner_pdb.py, test_native_edge_pdb.py, test_coordinates_and_tables.py, test_moves_and_cube.py, test_pocket_cube.py, test_exact_certificates.py, test_korf_native_evidence.py, test_thesis_audit.py, and the contract-assertion body of test_h48_oracle_contract.py; scanned by structure/grep test_h48_native.py, test_cli_and_results.py, test_nissy_external.py, test_rubikoptimal_external.py, test_symmetry.py and the 7 tests/infrastructure files (profiled mock density, read all test names of test_known_distance20_trimmed_prepass_sweep.py). Inspected scripts/generate_h48_oracle_contract.py and scripts/run_korf_native_evidence.py to classify what test assertions actually check. Ran pytest collection (700 tests, 68 marked native, 0 marked infrastructure), evaluated every skip gate on this machine (all open: /usr/bin/c++, native/build/optimal_solver, 6-edge/additive/7-edge PDBs, h48h0+h48h7 tables, phase1_sym_tables.bin, raw phase-1 table), and ran two read-only probes in /tmp-style one-liners (superflip generator reproduces the superflip cubie state; meet-in-the-middle proof that d(F R U R' U')=5). Verified h48 size constants in tests match real table file sizes on disk. NOT done (per assignment): did not run the test suite; did not read the ~16k lines of infrastructure tests line-by-line (sampled); did not fully read test_h48_native.py (1559 lines) or test_h48_oracle_contract.py beyond the main contract test; did not independently re-derive the edge-projection canonical-ball golden layer counts or the superflip phase-1 sym distance of 10; did not audit per-test runtime budgets or the saved artifacts the contract tests consume (another agent's scope).

areas.summary: The suite collects 700 tests. The non-native set (632 tests) finished in 158.03s with 617 passed, 1 failed, 14 skipped; the native set (68 tests) finished in 445.71s with 68 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped. Total wall time ~604s (~10 min). The one failure is tests/infrastructure/test_portfolio_oracle.py::test_portfolio_can_certify_upper_bound_with_native_korf_proof, which deterministically fails (reproduced in isolation) on `assert captured["nissy_heuristic"] is True` while the production config deliberately defaults native_korf_upper_bound_proof_nissy_heuristic to False for GPL-licensing reasons — a stale test expectation, not an unsoundness, but a red suite at HEAD (both files are unmodified relative to commit a57fa81). All 14 skips deduplicate to a single reason at tests/test_search_and_solvers.py:238: the randomized Python Korf/IDA* BFS-exact optimality test caps its BFS oracle at depth 4, so the 14 corpus scrambles with optimal distance >= 5 are never optimality-checked; this is documented as intentional, and the Python Korf path has no other non-mocked optimality test beyond distance 4. Crucially for the thesis claims, every native exactness/optimality gate actually ran and passed on this machine (no environment-driven skips): native IDA* vs BFS ground truth, the admissibility proof test (proves no solution at bound = d-1), 7-edge PDB admissibility vs BFS, symmetry-transposition variants, and two-phase-optimal vs the native Korf oracle. No hangs (longest test 123.87s), no pytest warnings, no collection errors.
areas.coverage: Reviewed/ran: full non-native set via `/opt/miniconda3/bin/python3 -m pytest -m "not native" -p no:cacheprovider -q -ra --durations=15` (632 tests, 158.03s, includes tests/infrastructure/), full native set via `-m "native"` (68 tests, 445.71s), plus an isolated deterministic rerun of the one failure (9.58s). All 700 collected tests were executed exactly once; nothing was excluded beyond the marker split. Verified skip reasons against test source (tests/test_search_and_solvers.py lines 80-243) and the failure against source (src/rubik_optimal/oracle.py lines 145-153, 3160; tests/infrastructure/test_portfolio_oracle.py lines 1194-1248); confirmed via `git status`/`git log` that both files involved in the failure are unmodified since baseline commit a57fa81. NOT done: pytest-timeout is not installed so runs had no per-test timeout enforcement (not needed — no hangs); did not run tests under parallelism (xdist), did not regenerate any tables or rebuild native binaries, did not audit test logic correctness beyond the failing/skipping tests (other reviewers' scope), did not inspect per-test warnings beyond the pytest summary lines (which reported zero warnings). Note the working tree is dirty (many modified src/, native/, tests/ files), so results reflect the working tree, not exactly commit a57fa81 — except the single failure, which is fully explained by committed, unmodified code.

areas.summary: I ran a seeded 24-scramble corpus (3 scrambles at each depth 1..8, random.Random(20260609), non-cancelling) through all main solver paths. Results that checked out: all 24 Thistlethwaite solutions and 23/24 Kociemba solutions replay to solved on the cube model; the native C++ optimal solver returned status=exact on 24/24 with every solution replaying to solved; the independent h48/nissy backend agreed with the native optimal length on 24/24; the exhaustive Python BFS oracle agreed on all 13 cases with optimal <= 4; the Python Korf IDA* agreed on 4 additional depth-4/5 cases; every optimal length was <= scramble depth; Kociemba length >= optimal on every case (equal on 21/24); mean Thistlethwaite length 11.0 >= mean Kociemba 4.96. Three distinct move engines (Python cubie model, native C++ solver, nissy) thus agree end-to-end, and no solver ever emitted a false "exact" or a non-solving solution. The problems found are all on the Kociemba two-phase path and on status taxonomy: the scoped Kociemba solver lets phase-1 candidate collection consume its entire timeout (so phase 2 is never attempted even when candidates exist), fails 5/5 random 18-move scrambles with default parameters, and mislabels some timeout/failed outcomes as "lower_bound". Superflip could not be re-certified by fresh h48 search within my ~120s probe budget (honest unknown_timeout both times), but the universal oracle returned a verified 20-move solution that I replayed to solved, and saved artifacts record exact 20 plus a 5200s depth-19 exhaustion proof.
areas.coverage: Reviewed/ran: solve_thistlethwaite_native_full, solve_kociemba_native_scoped (defaults + tuned budgets + 5 deep depth-18 scrambles), solve_korf_native_optimal (native/build/optimal_solver, 24 states), recognize_distance with h48_native (h48h0 on 24 states; h48h0/h48h7/h48h7+preload on superflip), exact_distance_bfs (13 states), solve_korf_ida (4 states), solve_universal_optimal (superflip), plus code reading of solvers/kociemba.py, solvers/optimal_native.py, distance.py, search/ida_star.py, benchmark.py, and the Kociemba rows of results/processed/benchmarks_seed_2026_thesis.csv and three saved superflip artifacts. NOT reviewed: correctness of PDB/table generation, coordinate/symmetry math, the C++ sources, h48/nissy backend internals, oracle race/portfolio plumbing, exact_certificates, 2x2 pocket solver, the LaTeX numbers beyond the Kociemba/superflip passages quoted; did not run the pytest suite; did not validate saved-artifact provenance beyond reading recorded values; optimal-agreement matrix only covers scramble depths 1-8 (per assignment) — Kociemba was additionally probed at depth 18 for robustness only; superflip optimality (lower-bound half) was not independently re-derived live. Scratch scripts: /tmp/xval_main.py, /tmp/xval_kociemba_deep.py, /tmp/xval_idx22_fix.py, /tmp/xval_superflip.py, /tmp/xval_superflip7.py, /tmp/xval_pykorf.py; per-case rows saved at /tmp/xval_rows.json.

areas.summary: No admissibility violation was found anywhere: across 700 checks on 100 seeded random states at depths 1-10 (exact d* from native/build/optimal_solver), 280,446 checks on the exhaustive 46,741-state depth<=4 ball, 8 exactly-solved depth-11..13 states, 16 deep states with exact/known distance (15 h48h7-verified solves at d*=16-18 plus superflip d*=20), and 23,561 phase-2-subgroup states for kociemba_phase2_lower_bound, every heuristic satisfied h(s) <= d*(s). Ground truth itself was triple-anchored: pure-Python BFS to depth 4 (ball sizes 1/18/243/3240/43239 match literature exactly; 40/40 agreement with the native solver), the repo's PDB-based optimal_solver, and the independent h48h0/h48h7 nissy-core backend (6/6 cross-engine agreement on mid-depth states); the repo's cube model also reproduces superflip exactly from the standard 20-move generator. PDB statistics are exactly right: the corner PDB (88,179,840 entries) has max 11, mean 8.764134 and the published Korf histogram {0:1,1:18,2:243,3:2874,4:28000,5:205416,6:1168516,7:5402628,8:20776176,9:45391616,10:15139616,11:64736}, solved entry 0; all eight 6-edge PDBs (42,577,920 entries, max 10, means 7.591-7.668), both 7-edge PDBs (510,935,040 entries, max 11, means 8.4491/8.4888) and both cost-partitioned PDBs (max 3, solved 0) have zero unvisited bytes, solved-coordinate value 0, and pass 3,900 local-consistency checks (|h(s)-h(s.m)| <= cost(m)). All results/processed/edge_pdb* and corner_pdb metadata JSONs match recomputed histograms and SHA-256 checksums byte-for-byte, including per-subset vs aggregate files and edge_pdb7_metadata.tex. The native solver's in-search heuristic combination (optimal_solver.cpp search_state_heuristic_full/bounded) is a MAX over admissible components with the cost-partitioned sum folded in correctly. The only issues found are three minor documentation/reporting drifts listed below; the load-bearing admissibility property held everywhere tested.
areas.coverage: Reviewed: all 8 public heuristics in src/rubik_optimal/search/heuristics.py (misplaced_cubie, coordinate_pruning, kociemba_phase2, combined_table, corner_pdb, edge_pdb6 max-of-8, edge_pdb7 max-of-2, additive cost-partitioned sum); the 13 PDB binary artifacts in data/generated/ (full histograms, headers, solved entries, checksums); 14 metadata JSONs + the .tex table in results/processed/; native heuristic combination logic in native/optimal_solver/optimal_solver.cpp (read, not exhaustively audited); tables/edge_pdb.py and tables/corner_pdb.py coordinate encoders (validated empirically end-to-end). Ground-truth engines used: optimal_solver (PDB IDA*), h48h0/h48h7 backend, pure-Python BFS. NOT reviewed: line-by-line audit of the native PDB generators (edge_pdb.cpp/corner_pdb) - artifacts validated empirically instead; the Kociemba phase1/phase2 .bin pruning tables and phase1_sym tables in data/generated/ (other reviewers' scope); h48 table contents (trusted only as a cross-check oracle, and it agreed with two independent oracles); nissy/dual-heuristic native code paths (read but not executed); exact-distance coverage at d*>=19 is limited - 5 of 20 deep random states and superflip exceeded my 60s/solve budget, so for those h was only checked against the scramble-length upper bound (all h <= 10 there, far below any plausible d*); benchmark-provenance and optimality-proof machinery (other assignments).

areas.summary: The results pipeline is in substantially better shape than expected: scripts/verify_results.py passes cleanly (52 rows, 0 errors), the benchmarks CSV is internally consistent (13 cases x 4 solvers, no duplicates, matches raw JSONL pair-for-pair, summary stats recompute exactly), and every headline number I recomputed from chapters 05/06_validation matches its saved artifact exactly, including: superflip certification 51.813928s/backend 44.591136s, trusted-preload 158.440286s, trusted-no-preload 91.629342s, batch 69.740540/6.033133=11.56x, trusted-table 36.643667/0.192970=189.894x, trusted-batch 38.198x, resident 4.638556/0.115502=40.16x, resident certification 72.071228s/77.822389s/154.755697s, API 2.664585s, nissy stress 23.734412s, portfolio 10.097333s/135.679618s/0.012885s, edge-PDB coverage 80 cases/14 improved, heuristic comparison 7 exact + 6 deeper rows, own-code superflip lower-bound proof 5200.8s (~87 min) with companion bound-18 248.5s, and pocket-cube 3,674,160/max 11/2,644 antipodes. My ground-truth spot-checks confirm the underlying artifacts are genuine: the corner PDB binary's distance histogram matches Korf's published distribution exactly (max 11, mean 8.7641), all 8 edge-PDB binaries match metadata checksums and sizes (340,623,936 B total), and the superflip certification solution independently re-verifies (20 moves, solves the true superflip built from the standard generator). The main problems are on the audit/provenance side: thesis_audit.py currently FAILS its own implementation acceptance because it requires six cloud-hardtail scripts that exist nowhere in the repo or its history, the HEAD-committed thesis_audit.json asserts checks that cannot be reproduced from any commit, every result artifact is tagged non-reproducible (dirty/uncommitted source), the auditor's solver-evidence checks largely trust self-reported passed/all_exact/verified flags, and the pytest suite would not catch a corrupted saved results file.

Ground-truth spot-checks: corner PDB binary (size, sha256, full histogram vs Korf's published distribution), all 8 edge-PDB binaries (sha256 + sizes), superflip certification row (state equals superflip from the standard generator; 20-move solution re-applied and solves), pocket-cube distribution (3,674,160/11/2,644), 7-edge PDB state counts (510,935,040 = 12!/5!*2^7). Read tests/test_thesis_audit.py and tests/test_cli_and_results.py in full (function level). areas.summary: The status discipline the chapter claims is genuinely enforced in code: solve_scramble_inverse and both Kociemba paths hard-code "non_exact" for verified solves, Thistlethwaite emits only "non_exact"/"failed", solve_auto_3x3 returns the first verified result without upgrading its status, and "exact" can only originate from exhaustive BFS or a fully completed IDA* iteration (truncated iterations break out at every level and return "timeout" with no solution, so a timeout can never be relabeled exact). Verification is a real replay: verify_solution parses the returned moves, applies them to the exact input state on the cube model, and checks is_solved plus physical validity. The artifact's internal cross-checks hold (BFS exact 3 == Korf exact 3 for the shallow case), all four LaTeX table rows match the JSON field-for-field, and inputs reconstruct deterministically (the 20-move scramble "R D2 F' B' L U2 L B' F' D2 L' B L' U' F U' R2 F' L B'" replays and its inverse solves). The serious problem is staleness: a clean rerun of the script with identical seed/profile/timeout under the committed code produces auto_solution_length 3 for shallow_sequence where the committed artifact and the thesis table say 4, and the artifact's recorded solver note is a string the current solver cannot emit — the artifact predates the committed Kociemba candidate-ranking code and was never regenerated. Additionally, the recorded lower bound 7 for the deep rows is now 9 under the working tree (the uncommitted 7-edge-PDB heuristic default; the committed 6-edge defaults alone already give 8). Lesser issues: the artifact stores no solution moves or input states, so the verified flags are self-attested rather than auditable; and the reported lower bound is needlessly weak (a completed depth-10 IDA* proves distance >= 11, but 7 was reported).

areas.summary: The Greek prose in these chapters is technically careful and almost all numeric claims check out against ground truth and repo artifacts: corner PDB 8!*3^7=88,179,840 (~88 MB file confirmed), eight 6-edge PDBs of 42,577,920 entries (~341 MB total, exactly matching data/generated/), CO 2187 / EO 2048 / UD-slice 495, phase-2 projections 8!/8!/4! with the 10-move subgroup, 18 HTM tokens, the Thistlethwaite G0>G1>G2>G3 chain description, Pocket Cube 7!*3^6=3,674,160, and correct God's-number citations. The new 01_introduction contribution claiming a native (no H48/Nissy) proof that superflip distance is exactly 20 is fully supported: native_reid_optimal_superflip_probe_seed_2026_target19.json has status=lower_bound, proves_no_solution_at_or_below_target=true, timed_out=false, uses_h48_or_nissy=false, a 16-symmetry FlipUDSlice phase-1 table (sym_count=16, 64,430 classes, max distance 12) and three-axis Reid pruning; the 20-move upper solution saved in native_superflip_upper_bound_probe_seed_2026_compact20.json (upper_solution_verified=true) was independently re-verified here with the repo's own cube model (it solves the standard superflip in 20 HTM moves). thesis/main.pdf is fresh: it contains the newly added superflip sentence, is newer than every .tex source, and is md5-identical to root main.pdf. The real problems are in Appendix A: its prescribed verification chain fails at the current tree (pytest has 1 failing test; thesis_audit's implementation acceptance gate fails because six required scripts moved to scripts/experimental/), plus several smaller doc/code drifts (four-vs-eight edge PDB count in ch. 05, stale "within controlled limits" Thistlethwaite phrasing in the intro, incomplete schema field list in Appendix B, wrong implied locations for the Pocket Cube artifacts). Trivia folded here: 02_background.tex:18 uses ";" (which is the Greek question mark) as a separator after a \cite, rendering as a mid-sentence question mark; 00_front_matter retains expected institutional placeholders ("© 20XX", committee names, registration number) that the audit and Appendix C already track as submission blockers, so they are consistent rather than contradictory.

areas.summary: The CLI's exactness discipline is fundamentally sound: solver statuses (exact/non_exact/lower_bound/timeout/failed/not_applicable) are passed through to JSON untouched, "exact" is only emitted together with an independent verify_solution() re-check, the oracle command's "distance" field is populated only for status=="exact" (cli.py:132), and oracle exits 1 unless every row is exact AND verified. Distance recognition correctly downgrades to "lower_bound"/"unknown_timeout" with explicit disclaimers (probed on superflip: value 8, kind lower_bound, honest notes). Cross-checks agree (Python Korf IDA*, native optimal, and H48 all give distance 6 for the same 7-move scramble). Every command and script named in thesis/chapters/d_cli_reference.tex exists with the documented flags (no moved-path failures like the earlier thesis_audit one), and the appendix demo commands (solve --solver h48-oracle, oracle --h48-solver h48h7/h48h0, distance, verify) all execute and produce the documented JSON. source_state.py stamps the current dirty repo correctly ("3226442+dirty", 97 entries) and handles clean/unborn/non-git cases correctly; runtime.run_process_tree really kills descendant process groups on timeout and flags timed_out (probed), and its script consumers honor the flag. The main problems are completeness rather than soundness: the scoped native-Kociemba leg of `solve` cannot solve typical random 20-move states within its documented defaults (phase-1 candidate collection times out even at 10-20s; budgets 10<12 and 14<18 are below the phase diameters), and the auto chain silently masks this with the optional GPL pip kociemba package (installed here, but not part of the dev extra the appendix's clean-final-run recipe installs) or a ~33-move Thistlethwaite fallback. There are also exit-code inconsistencies (solve always exits 0 even on timeout/failed) and a hidden trusted-table default in oracle --universal.

Executed probes (<60s each, outputs to /tmp only): scramble, facelets, verify, solve (auto on 6- and 20-move scrambles, native-kociemba on a random 20-move facelet state at timeouts 10/20s, korf, optimal-native, h48-oracle with the 3.5GB h48h7 table), distance (BFS-exact, superflip lower-bound, parse error), oracle (h48h0 batch --jsonl, invalid-state exit code), a clean-environment simulation with pip kociemba blocked, source_state from /tmp against dirty/clean/unborn/non-git repos, and run_process_tree descendant-kill/timeout. areas.summary: The closure mathematics is implemented correctly and is defensively verified at every derivation step. I verified from first principles and by probe that: (a) the conjugation homomorphism sigma(s.m)=sigma(s).sigma(m) holds for all 48 symmetries on random states/sequences, including the 24 reflections where reflected clockwise quarter turns correctly map to counterclockwise turns (no sign bug — the move map is derived empirically with a uniqueness check in symmetry.py:172-185); (b) the inverse closure (exact_certificates.py:225-243) and rotation closure (245-277) preserve length and re-verify every derived solution before insertion; (c) a full replay of all 7,562 stored certificates (160 direct, 3,634 symmetry, 157 inverse, 3,611 inverse_symmetry) found 0 invalid solutions, 0 length mismatches, and 0 length conflicts between artifacts (verified with an instrumented loader); (d) an independent BFS to depth 5 from solved proved optimality for all 241 certificates of length <=5, and the BFS layer sizes (18/243/3240/43239/574908) match the known HTM ball counts; (e) the superflip is stored at exactly 20 and the thesis-cited certificate-cache row traces to a real 77.8s H48 solve; (f) all 2,086 rows in the three saved closure artifacts replay correctly; (g) the oracle's symmetry-batch backends map rotated solutions back via inverse_transform_sequence and re-verify on the original state before claiming exact. The one substantive weakness is the trust root: 125 of the 160 direct certificates — and through closure ~96% of the store — inherit status="exact" from a third-party benchmark file label with no local optimality proof, and that caveat is progressively erased as certificates flow through the store and oracle notes. None of the closure or benchmark-label tables are cited in the thesis chapters, which limits the blast radius to saved artifacts.
areas.coverage: Reviewed in full: scripts/run_certificate_cache_symmetry_closure.py, scripts/run_certificate_cache_inverse_closure.py, scripts/run_universal_symmetry_oracle.py, scripts/import_nissy_benchmark_certificates.py, src/rubik_optimal/exact_certificates.py, src/rubik_optimal/symmetry.py, src/rubik_optimal/moves.py (inverse helpers), src/rubik_optimal/verify.py; oracle.py certificate/symmetry paths in detail (lines 2440-2880, 3295-3565, 3583-3870 solve, 3872-4230 solve_many head, 4508-4630 nissy symmetry batch, 5156-5225 rubikoptimal symmetry mapping). Ran 6 behavioral probes (all in /tmp, repo untouched): 48-symmetry conjugation homomorphism + reflection sign check; full 7,562-certificate replay with instrumented conflict detection; BFS-to-depth-5 independent optimality proof for all length<=5 certificates; end-to-end UniversalOptimalOracle solve of 5 random closure-derived states plus superflip; replay of all 2,086 saved closure-artifact rows; thesis citation grep across all 44 \input{tables/...} in thesis/chapters. NOT reviewed: optimality soundness of the native H48/nissy backends and the h48 lower-bound admissibility behind upper-lower certificates (separate trust root, out of scope); oracle.py solve_many tail 4230-4496 line-by-line (skimmed, pattern-consistent with the verified-exact gating elsewhere); no independent optimality verification of any certificate with length >=6 (the 125 benchmark parents at 16-20 would each need minutes of native search); learned-certificate jsonl concurrency behavior not exercised.

areas.summary: The benchmark scripts are structurally fair on the basics: in every A/B comparison both arms solve the identical states with identical thread counts and per-case timeout budgets, the batch/resident walls include their own process spawn + table load/check, and the thesis (04_implementation.tex:96-98, 05_experiments.tex, 06_validation.tex:39) discloses unusually well that the 10x+ numbers are table-load/table-check amortization, not search speedups, and that the 0.012885s portfolio row is a certificate-cache hit without a new search. The 11.56x batch number (12 full-scan processes vs 1) is structurally sound. The real methodological problem is ordering: in all three A/B benchmarks the fast arm runs second in the same process, so the one-time cold page-in of the 3.8 GB mmapped table (~3.4-3.6s for the ~43k random lookups of the test state, observed directly in per-row data) is charged entirely to the slow arm. This inflates the trusted-batch 38.198x to roughly 4x and the resident 40.16x to roughly 11x in steady state, and makes the headline 189.894x order-dependent (order-swapped totals would be ~10x), while the thesis sentences attribute these totals to per-process/table-setup overhead. Secondary issues: all timings are single ordered runs quoted at microsecond precision with pass-gates that require minimum speedups; several thesis artifacts were generated by an older script revision (missing keys the current scripts always write); trusted mode's per-call validation never hashes the table bytes; and an uncited passed=false artifact shows superflip timing out at the same 90s budget quoted for the 51.8s headline run.
areas.coverage: Reviewed in full: scripts/benchmark_h48_batch_overhead.py, benchmark_h48_trusted_table.py, benchmark_h48_resident_oracle.py, benchmark_h48_solver_levels.py, run_fast_optimal_oracle_api.py, run_portfolio_optimal_oracle.py, run_h48_oracle_cli.py, run_h48_oracle_certification.py; the timed-region implementations in src/rubik_optimal/solvers/h48_native.py (solve_h48_native_optimal lines 1418-1576, solve_h48_native_batch 2231-2453, H48NativeOracleSession 250-380), src/rubik_optimal/tables/h48.py (validate_trusted_h48_table 279-333), src/rubik_optimal/exact_certificates.py (86-180), native/h48_backend/h48_backend.c (--skip-table-check / nissy_checkdata at line 602), scripts/verify_results.py h48 checksum block (314-334); all 16 matching JSON artifacts in results/processed (batch overhead x2, trusted-table, resident, resident-certification, 5 certification variants, 2 CLI, API, 6 portfolio) including per-row runtimes; and the quote sites in thesis/chapters/04_implementation.tex, 05_experiments.tex (lines 178-318, 370-400), 06_validation.tex:39, 06_discussion.tex, 07_conclusions.tex. Verified all six headline ratios recompute from artifact fields. areas.summary: The pocket-cube subsystem is correct from first principles; I found no critical or major issues. Verified: (1) the quotient construction is sound — DBL (corner index 6 in src/rubik_optimal/cube.py) is fixed at home position/orientation, U/R/F and their powers provably fix it while D/L/B move it, and PocketState.apply_move equals the 3x3 cubie-model corner restriction on 300 random full-space states x 9 moves; group orders check out (quarter turns order 4, doubles order 2, sexy-move order 6). (2) coord()/from_coord() is a bijection over 7!*3^6 = 3,674,160 (22k random roundtrips both directions, boundary coords 0/728/729/3674159, out-of-range rejected). (3) A full rerun of `generate_pocket_cube.py --profile thesis --seed 2026` into /tmp reproduced the saved JSON byte-identically (modulo runtime_seconds) and both thesis .tex tables byte-identically; the distribution matches the published 2x2x2 HTM table (max 11, 2,644 antipodes, sums to 3,674,160), and depths 0-6 (1/9/54/321/1847/9992/50136) were independently reproduced by a cubie-level BFS that bypasses all pocket coordinate/table code. (4) All five representative solutions replay to solved and their lengths equal the exhaustive-BFS distances (optimality confirmed independently of the solver); the per-query BFS in pocket/optimal.py with same-face pruning and coord-keyed visited set provably preserves distances (merge/cancel argument), and the coordinate move tables match cubie moves on 3000 random full-space states (beyond the repo's own depth-5 check). (5) Thesis wording in chapters 01/03/04/05/06/07 is carefully scoped — the 2x2x2 is consistently framed as a supporting exhaustive example, explicitly "not a substitute for the 3x3 track", with no overgeneralization. Remaining issues are minor: a self-acknowledged missing primary citation for God's Number 11, a small implementation-description mismatch in chapter 04, an unused --seed parameter embedded in artifact filenames, and a status-taxonomy quirk in the solver facade. pytest tests/test_pocket_cube.py: 7 passed.
areas.coverage: Reviewed line-by-line: src/rubik_optimal/pocket/cube.py, pocket/optimal.py, pocket/tables.py, src/rubik_optimal/solvers/pocket_cube.py, scripts/generate_pocket_cube.py, tests/test_pocket_cube.py, src/rubik_optimal/coordinates/permutation.py (rank/unrank), moves.py same_face, the corner constants/moves of src/rubik_optimal/cube.py, results/processed/pocket_cube_summary_seed_2026_{thesis,quick}.json, thesis/tables/pocket_cube_{distribution,representatives}{,_thesis,_quick}.tex, verify_results.py pocket section (lines 178-220), and the pocket-related passages of thesis chapters 00/01/03/04/05/06_discussion/06_validation/07.
